title: Screen Time and Learning: Finding the Balance
lead: Online tutoring adds screen time. The question isn't whether screens are good or bad—it's how to use them for learning without burning out.

sections.title: Not All Screen Time Is Equal
content: A focused tutoring session is different from passive scrolling. Interactive, purposeful screen use—video calls, problem-solving, collaborative work—engages the brain differently than passive consumption.

sections.title: Build in Breaks
content: After a 45-minute session, step away. Look out a window, grab water, move. Back-to-back screen blocks lead to fatigue and diminishing returns.

sections.title: Protect Sleep
content: Avoid screens right before bed when possible. If evening tutoring is the only option, dim the screen and keep the room lit to reduce blue light impact.

sections.title: Quality Over Quantity
content: An hour of focused tutoring can be more valuable than two hours of distracted studying. Prioritize intentional use over total minutes.

cta.title: Make Screen Time Count
cta.text: GuruForU sessions are structured and purposeful—focused learning, not mindless scrolling.

faq.question: Is all screen time the same?
faq.answer: No. Focused tutoring—video calls, problem-solving, collaborative work—engages the brain differently than passive scrolling. Interactive, purposeful use is not the same as mindless consumption.

faq.question: How can I prevent screen fatigue during learning?
faq.answer: Build in breaks. After a 45-minute session, step away—look out a window, grab water, move. Back-to-back screen blocks lead to fatigue and diminishing returns.

faq.question: Should we avoid screens before bed?
faq.answer: Yes when possible. If evening tutoring is the only option, dim the screen and keep the room lit to reduce blue light impact on sleep.

faq.question: Does more screen time mean better learning?
faq.answer: No. Quality over quantity. An hour of focused tutoring can be more valuable than two hours of distracted studying.
